name: FXIFY
description_short: Broker-backed prop firm offering 1-step, 2-step, and 3-step evaluations with 300+ instruments, EA support, and payouts within 3 business days.
description_long:
## What FXIFY Is and Who Runs It

FXIFY is a broker-backed proprietary trading firm launched in 2023 and operated by FXIFY Solutions Limited out of London, United Kingdom. Unlike many prop firms that run purely simulated environments, FXIFY is structurally tied to FXPIG, an established institutional-grade broker, which gives the firm access to real brokerage infrastructure, deeper liquidity relationships, and a more credible execution stack than the average white-labeled prop shop.

The firm sits in the crowded prop firm category alongside FTMO, Topstep, The5ers, and Apex Trader Funding, and has aggressively differentiated itself through flexible challenge formats, generous scaling, and a strategy-agnostic rulebook. In roughly two years FXIFY has moved from a newcomer to one of the more visible names in the space, largely on the back of aggressive marketing, frequent promotional discounts, and a reputation for paying traders on time.

## Core Features and What Makes FXIFY Different

FXIFY offers four distinct paths to funded capital: a 1-Step Challenge, 2-Step Challenge, 3-Step Challenge, and an Instant Funding program. That range is broader than most competitors, and it is the single most important reason traders shortlist the firm. Whether you are a scalper who hates time limits, a swing trader who wants a lower-cost entry, or a funded veteran who wants to skip evaluation entirely, there is a lane.

The platform supports **MT4, MT5, and DXTrade**, with over 300 tradable instruments spanning forex, indices, commodities, futures, and more than 80 cryptocurrency CFDs. Crypto coverage is genuinely deep — most prop firms treat crypto as an afterthought, while FXIFY runs dedicated crypto plans. Expert Advisors, algorithmic systems, martingale, grid strategies, and news trading are all permitted, which puts it ahead of firms like Topstep or FTMO on strategy flexibility.

Payouts are advertised as processed within 3 business days, with a First Withdrawal On Demand feature that lets funded traders request their first payout without waiting for the standard cycle. The firm also offers scaling up to $4,000,000 in simulated capital, bi-weekly payout options, and add-ons like a 90% profit split upgrade and Performance Protect (which cushions a losing day against a hard breach).

## FXIFY Pricing Breakdown

FXIFY pricing is tiered by evaluation format and account size, and the entry point is competitive:

- **3-Step Challenge:** Starts at **$59** — the cheapest way in, but requires clearing three phases before funding. Accounts run $5K to $200K, with an 80% profit split.
- **1-Step Challenge:** Starts at **$75**. Single evaluation phase, no time limit, accounts from $5K to $400K, 80% split. This is the format most retail traders default to.
- **2-Step Challenge:** Starts at **$99**. Two phases, accounts from $10K to $400K, 80% split, EA and algo trading fully allowed.
- **Instant Funding:** Starts at **$899**. No evaluation, immediate account access, $25K+ sizes, 80% split.

The sticker prices look attractive, but the real cost picture changes once you stack the optional add-ons. Upgrading to a **90% profit split**, adding **bi-weekly payouts**, or purchasing a **leverage boost** can quickly push a $99 challenge north of $200. FXIFY runs near-constant promotional discounts (30% off, World Cup sales, new customer codes), which strongly suggests the list prices are aspirational and the discounted prices are the real market price. Wait for a code before buying — you almost never need to pay full sticker at FXIFY.

## The Honest Pros

FXIFY genuinely does several things well, and pretending otherwise would be dishonest.

The **broker-backed model via FXPIG** is the strongest structural differentiator in the FXIFY offering. It reduces some of the counterparty anxiety that plagues purely simulated firms, and it means execution quality on funded accounts tends to be closer to what you would get at a legitimate broker than at a purely synthetic prop shop.

**Strategy flexibility** is unusually generous. EAs, martingale, grid, and news trading are all explicitly permitted — try that at most US-focused futures prop firms and you will be shown the door. For algorithmic and systematic traders, this alone can be the deciding factor.

**Payout reliability** has been consistently good. The 3-business-day processing window is real, and the First Withdrawal On Demand feature is a legitimate improvement over the typical 14-day or 30-day first-payout wait at competitors.

The **crypto CFD depth** — 80+ instruments plus dedicated crypto plans — is well ahead of FTMO, Topstep, and The5ers. And the **scaling ceiling of $4M** in simulated capital, while unlikely to be reached by most traders, is one of the higher published ceilings in the industry.

## The Honest Cons

FXIFY has real limitations that traders should weigh before buying.

**The company is young.** Founded in 2023, FXIFY does not yet have the multi-cycle track record of FTMO (2015) or The5ers (2016). Prop firms have a habit of tightening rules, quietly changing payout policies, or collapsing entirely once growth slows, and two years is not enough data to fully judge how FXIFY will behave under stress.

**There is no dedicated FXIFY mobile app.** Everything runs through MT4, MT5, or DXTrade's mobile clients. That is fine for most traders but disappointing compared to firms building proprietary experiences.

**Add-on pricing is where the real money is made.** The advertised entry prices look competitive, but the 90% split upgrade, bi-weekly payouts, and leverage boost all cost extra. Traders who want the "full" FXIFY experience end up paying significantly more than the headline number, and the aggressive discounting suggests margins are healthy on the add-on stack.

**Instant Funding is expensive.** At $899 minimum, the no-evaluation path is one of the pricier ways to skip the challenge in the industry, and the 80% split is not meaningfully better than what you get after clearing a $99 challenge.

**Promotional pricing dependence** is a subtle red flag. When a firm runs discounts nearly every month, the list price is essentially fictional, and it can make comparing FXIFY to competitors on a like-for-like basis difficult.

## Who Should Use FXIFY (And Who Should Not)

FXIFY is a strong fit for **algorithmic and EA traders** who need a firm that will not penalize automation, **crypto CFD traders** who want dedicated crypto plans instead of a single BTC/USD pair, **multi-strategy traders** who use martingale or grid systems that are banned elsewhere, and traders who specifically want a **broker-backed evaluation** rather than a fully simulated environment.

FXIFY is a poor fit for traders who need a **polished proprietary mobile app**, traders who want a firm with a **decade-plus track record** and reputational scar tissue, and traders on a strict budget who cannot stomach the add-on stack. If you want the cheapest possible path to a funded account with no bells and whistles, competitors like The5ers or Alpha Capital may be a better spend.

## FXIFY vs FTMO and The5ers

**FTMO** remains the gold standard for reputation, dashboards, and analytics. FTMO is stricter on strategy (no martingale, tighter news rules) and slightly more expensive at entry, but the multi-year payout track record and the depth of trader analytics tools are hard to beat. FXIFY wins on strategy flexibility, price flexibility via promos, and crypto depth. FTMO wins on trust and polish.

**The5ers** takes a more conservative, long-hold-friendly approach with a well-regarded scaling model. It is a better fit for swing traders and lower-frequency systematic traders. FXIFY is broader and more aggressive — more account sizes, more challenge formats, more add-ons, more discounts. If your strategy is slow and steady, The5ers is probably a cleaner match. If your strategy is high-frequency, algorithmic, or crypto-heavy, FXIFY is the stronger pick.

## The Bottom Line on FXIFY

FXIFY is a legitimate, well-executed prop firm with a real broker relationship, unusually flexible rules, and reliably fast payouts. The 2023 founding date is worth taking seriously — you are paying to trade with a firm still in its adolescence, and the industry has a pattern of firms tightening rules once they hit scale.

For algorithmic traders, crypto CFD traders, and anyone whose strategy gets banned at more conservative shops, FXIFY is one of the better options available in 2026. Just wait for a promo code before you buy, budget carefully around the add-on stack, and do not treat the advertised sticker prices as the real cost of the platform.
